prompt:
A close-up of a 3D printer’s X-axis gantry and belt tensioner, with a gloved hand using a hex key or small wrench to adjust the belt tension. The toothed belt runs clearly across the scene, and a small icon-only gauge sticker or screen on the printer frame hints at proper tension without any readable text. In the blurred background, a few printed parts with slightly wavy edges sit on the workbench, suggesting why belt tension matters for sharp prints.

Near-future solarpunk workshop aesthetic with warm lighting, wooden surfaces, and a plant or two in the background. Photorealistic 512×512 rendering, no brand logos or legible markings. The composition should make it obvious that the task is safely tightening a loose X-axis belt to keep prints crisp.
